---
title: Lesson Template
description: Authoring pattern for edu-math lessons.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- State what the learner should be able to do.
- Use verbs that can be checked: compute, identify, explain, graph, solve.
:::

::: {.prerequisites}
### Prerequisites

- List earlier ideas the learner should know.
- Link to earlier lessons when possible.
:::

## Motivation

Explain why the concept matters before introducing notation.

## Concept

Use plain language and a small example.

## Definition

Introduce formal notation.

## Visual Model

Use either a Python plot from `src/edumath` or a browser-side widget when the
rendered documentation must stay interactive without a live Python kernel.

## Worked Example

Show every step.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="false"}
## Worked example pattern

1. State the problem.
2. Name the method.
3. Show each algebraic, numeric, graphical, or logical step.
4. Check the final answer.
:::

## Common Mistakes

Name the mistake and show how to avoid it.

## Practice

Give short exercises and quizzes. Every exercise should have an answer reveal.

::: {.practice-box}
1. Write a short exercise.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: Give the final answer first.

Then explain the reasoning step by step.
:::

## Python Connection

Show how the concept maps to reusable code.

## Summary

- Restate the main idea.
- Name the most important formula or technique.
- Link to the next lesson.
